description:
src/lib.rs:83-90 — only `?field=` is checked and rejected. Any other query param is silently ignored.

Trigger: user types `secretx:doppler:proj/cfg/name?feild=pw` (typo). Or the user assumes Doppler supports a parameter that another backend supports (e.g. `?version=`, `?format=`).
Bad behavior: backend silently ignores the param, returns the full secret. The user sees correct-looking output and ships a config that ignores their stated intent. They file a confusing bug later.

Fix: after rejecting `?field=`, iterate over all params and reject any that the doppler backend does not recognize (currently: none). Use SecretError::InvalidUri with a message that names the unknown key. Sibling backends likely have the same gap; flag here for this crate only.

Note this conflicts with future-extensibility: if a future doppler-backend version adds `?include_dynamic=true`, older clients passing it will fail. Mitigation: document this as the contract, and version-bump when adding new params.
title: from_uri silently accepts unknown query parameters (typo footgun)
